task_completion.description: The agent navigated the axial view to the sella turcica.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The crosshairs in the axial view panel (usually top-left) are moved from their default position to the center of the skull base, showing the sella turcica.

task_completion.description: The agent navigated the sagittal view to the sella turcica.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The crosshairs in the sagittal view panel (usually top-right) are moved to the center of the head, clearly showing the saddle-shaped depression of the sella turcica.

task_completion.description: The agent navigated the coronal view to the sella turcica.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The crosshairs in the coronal view panel (usually bottom-left) are moved to the center of the head, focusing on the skull base.

task_completion.description: The agent exported the axial view as a PNG file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or export action is visible, initiated from the axial view panel, saving the file as 'axial_sella.png' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: The agent exported the sagittal view as a PNG file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or export action is visible, initiated from the sagittal view panel, saving the file as 'sagittal_sella.png' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: The agent exported the coronal view as a PNG file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or export action is visible, initiated from the coronal view panel, saving the file as 'coronal_sella.png' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the InVesalius application window, including clicking on view panels and using the built-in export functionality.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely navigated the 3D views rather than exporting default slices.
integrity.visual_evidence: The crosshairs and slice indices in the view panels change over time, indicating active scrolling or clicking to locate the specific anatomical structure.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent opening a terminal to run Python scripts, ImageMagick commands, or other CLI tools to generate or rename the required PNG files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses the CT Cranium dataset in InVesalius. The sella turcica is a real anatomical structure: a saddle-shaped depression in the sphenoid bone at the skull base, located roughly at the center of the head in all three dimensions. The three orthogonal planes are Axial (transverse/horizontal), Sagittal (left-right), and Coronal (front-back). The agent must navigate the crosshairs to this structure and export the three views as PNG files using the InVesalius GUI. Look for save dialogs targeting '/home/ga/Documents/' with the specific filenames 'axial_sella.png', 'sagittal_sella.png', and 'coronal_sella.png'.
